message:
Release v0.3.1: Fix POSIX compatibility in polyglot wrapper

Updated polyglot hook wrapper example and documentation to use
POSIX-compliant syntax for better compatibility with dash (default
/bin/sh on Ubuntu/Debian).

Changes:
- examples/full-featured-plugin/hooks/run-hook.cmd: Use $0 instead of ${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-$0}
- skills/developing-claude-code-plugins/references/polyglot-hooks.md: Updated example and added POSIX compliance note
- Updated version to 0.3.1 in all required files

This matches the fix made in superpowers v3.6.2.

@@ -5,6 +5,15 @@ All notable changes to the superpowers-developing-for-claude-code plugin will be
 The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/),
 and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).
 
+## [0.3.1] - 2025-12-03
+
+### Fixed
+- Updated polyglot hook wrapper to use POSIX-compliant syntax
+  - Changed `${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-$0}` to `$0` in run-hook.cmd example
+  - Prevents "Bad substitution" errors on Ubuntu/Debian systems where /bin/sh is dash
+  - Updated polyglot-hooks.md documentation with explanation of POSIX compliance requirement
+  - Matches fix from superpowers v3.6.2
+
 ## [0.3.0] - 2025-12-02
 
 ### Added
